sections.paragraph:
fut. יִמְרֹר to be contumacious, rebellious, to move sedition, Gen 14:4 followed by בְּ 2Ki 18:7, 20 2Ki 18:20 24:1 , 20 2Ki 24:20and עַל of the person rebelled against, Neh 2:19 2Ch 13:6 more rarely with an acc. (comp. מָרָה), Jos 22:16 Job 24:13 (see below). מָרַד בַּיהֹוָה to rebel against Jehovah (by worshipping idols), Jos 22:16, sqq.; Eze 2:3 Dan 9:9. Poet. מֹרְדֵי־אֹור those who oppose the light, the enemies of light, Job 24:13. (Syriac ܡܪܕ id. Arabic to be obstinate, contumacious. Kindred is מָרָה.)

The derivatives immediately follow, except the pr.n. נִמְרֹד.
